Title: Lineage-specific gene radiations underlie the evolution of novel betalain pigmentation in Caryophyllales

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates betalain pathway genes DODA-α and CYP76AD1-α in Beta vulgaris.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Discussion from prior work (Hatlestad et al., 2014) as a linked regulator on chromosome 2; not experimentally analyzed in this paper.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The R locus is cited as a MYB regulator of DODA-α and CYP76AD1-α in Beta vulgaris. 2. It is linked to the betalain-related genomic region on chromosome 2. 3. This paper does not functionally test the R locus, so it is background rather than a core experimental gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the 4,5-extradiol cleavage of L-DOPA to form betalamic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms a core chromophore precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme; betalain-specific control point inferred
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Betalain-specific DODA-α homologs are associated with betalain pigmentation; loss or downregulation correlates with anthocyanic lineages lacking betalains.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Absent or reduced in many anthocyanic taxa; retained in betalainic taxa
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected broadly in betalainic taxa, rarely in anthocyanic taxa
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss/downregulation associated with absence of betalain pigmentation in anthocyanic lineages
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics; genome analysis; GenBank accessions KR376141–KR376346; SRA BioProject PRJNA280277
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All functionally characterized betalain DODAs from Beta vulgaris, Mirabilis jalapa, and Portulaca grandiflora map to the DODA-α clade. Diagnostic residues include P183 and W227; positive selection at sites 82, 102, and 115. In Beta vulgaris, a betalain-related DODA-α copy lies within ~50 kb of CYP76AD1-α on chromosome 2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering betalain production, especially combined with CYP76AD1-α; clade-specific isoforms should be chosen because sister DODA-β copies are not implicated in betalain synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DODA underwent an early duplication yielding DODA-α and DODA-β. 2. All experimentally validated betalain DODA genes fall in the DODA-α clade. 3. DODA-α is largely absent or downregulated in anthocyanic lineages, supporting a betalain-specific neofunctionalization. 4. DODA-α shows diagnostic residues and signatures of positive selection consistent with functional divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic mapping of functionally characterized orthologs; comparative transcriptome/genome presence-absence analysis; genomic colocalization analysis; positive selection analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of cyclo-DOPA from L-DOPA in the betalain pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms a core precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme; betalain-specific control point inferred
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Betalain-specific CYP76AD1-α homologs are associated with betalain pigmentation; absence in anthocyanic lineages correlates with loss of betalain pathway capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Absent or reduced in anthocyanic taxa; retained in betalainic taxa
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in betalainic taxa, not recovered from anthocyanic transcriptomes examined
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss/downregulation associated with evolutionary exclusion of betalains in anthocyanic lineages
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics; genome analysis; GenBank accessions KR376350–KR376500; SRA BioProject PRJNA280277
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All functionally characterized betalain-related CYP76AD1 genes from Beta vulgaris, Mirabilis jalapa, and Amaranthus cruentus map to CYP76AD1-α. Diagnostic residues include H111, T114, T131, L186, I207, D241, and V275; positive selection identified at 111, 114, 131, 186, 241, and 275. In Beta vulgaris, a betalain-related CYP76AD1-α copy is within ~50 kb of DODA-α on chromosome 2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for introducing betalain pigmentation, especially stacked with DODA-α; betalain-specific α-clade genes are preferable to β/γ paralogs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AD1 underwent early duplications yielding α, β, and γ clades. 2. All experimentally validated betalain CYP76AD1 genes fall in the α clade. 3. CYP76AD1-α is not recovered from anthocyanic taxa, supporting betalain-specific neofunctionalization and pathway loss in anthocyanic lineages. 4. Positive selection and diagnostic residues support functional divergence of the α clade.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic mapping of functionally characterized orthologs; comparative transcriptome/genome presence-absence analysis; genomic colocalization analysis; positive selection analysis
